perturbation.original_text: 15.Signing this Agreement does not bar disclosures to Congress or to an authorized official of an executive agency or the Department of Justice that are essential to reporting a substantial violation of law.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. I hereby acknowledge that I have received a security indoctrination concerning the nature and protection of information to which I have been provided conditional access, including the procedures to be followed in ascertaining whether other persons to whom I contemplate disclosing this information have been approved for access to it, and that I understand these procedures. 15.Signing this Agreement does not bar disclosures to Congress or to an authorized official of an executive agency or the Department of Justice that are essential to reporting a substantial violation of law.
perturbation.explanation: Relocating the exception for disclosures to Congress and law enforcement to the section concerning security indoctrination reduces its visibility and diminishes its impact, as it's now mixed with clauses concerning authorized access and security.
perturbation.law_explanation: This federal statute guarantees the right of federal employees to petition Congress. By embedding the statement about disclosures to Congress within a paragraph about security, its prominence is reduced and its impact diminished, potentially discouraging or intimidating employees from exercising their right to communicate with Congress about potential wrongdoing.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 5 U.S. Code § 7211 - Employees’ right to petition Congress | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 5 U.S. Code § 7211 - Employees’ right to petition Congress U.S. Code Notes The right of employees, individually or collectively, to petition Congress or a Member of Congress , or to furnish information to either House of Congress , or to a committee or Member thereof, may not be interfered with or denied. (Added Pub. L. 95–454, title VII, § 703(a)(3) , Oct. 13, 1978 , 92 Stat. 1217 .) Editorial Notes Prior Provisions Provisions of this section were contained in section 7102 of this title prior to the general amendment of chapter 71 of this title by Pub. L. 95–454, title VII, § 701 , Oct. 13, 1978 , 92 Stat. 1191 . Statutory Notes and Related Subsidiaries Effective Date Section effective 90 days after Oct. 13, 1978 , see section 907 of Pub. L. 95–454 , set out as an Effective Date of 1978 Amendment note under section 1101 of this title . U.S. Code Toolbox Law about... perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts 5 U.S. Code § 7211. The original text clearly states that signing the agreement does not bar disclosures to Congress. By embedding this statement within a section concerning security indoctrination, the changed text diminishes its prominence and impact. This could potentially discourage employees from communicating with Congress about potential wrongdoing, thus interfering with their right to petition Congress, as stated in the law snippet: 'The right of employees, individually or collectively, to petition Congress or a Member of Congress , or to furnish information to either House of Congress , or to a committee or Member thereof, may not be interfered with or denied.'
